description: Definition of Done #3 specifies `bun run test:dom -- --coverage` as the branch-coverage gate for the three pure modules (editorPredicates.ts, editorReducer.ts, debounceSchedule.ts). This invocation is incapable of producing the required >=95% branch coverage on the pure modules: vitest's include pattern (vitest.config.ts lines 22-25) excludes `*.test.ts` and `*.prop.test.ts`, so the pure-tier unit and property tests never run under vitest. The DOM-tier vitest tests do transitively import editorReducer.ts and editorPredicates.ts via EditorPanel.svelte, so SOME branches are reached, but the systematic branch enumeration provided by Tier 1 boundary tests and Tier 2 fast-check properties (ref CRIT-705 and CRIT-703) executes only under `bun test`. Bun's native coverage flag is `bun test --coverage`, not `bun run test:dom -- --coverage`. CRIT-713's passThreshold correctly identifies this and uses `bun test --coverage src/lib/editor/__tests__/...` plus an alternative gate; DoD #3 directly contradicts CRIT-713.

evidence.snippet: 3. Branch coverage: `bun run test:dom -- --coverage` reports branch coverage ≥ 95% per file for the three pure modules.

suggestedFix: Rewrite DoD #3 to mirror CRIT-713's passThreshold: (a) `cd promptnotes && bun test --coverage src/lib/editor/__tests__/editor*.test.ts src/lib/editor/__tests__/debounceSchedule.test.ts src/lib/editor/__tests__/prop/` reports >=95% branch coverage per file on editorPredicates.ts, editorReducer.ts, debounceSchedule.ts in stdout; OR (b) the documented equivalent gate when Bun coverage stdout is unavailable (CRIT-703 passes AND canonical purity grep returns zero hits AND tsc --strict --noUncheckedIndexedAccess is clean).
